items.title: Girl's suspected abductor had been out of jail for just six days, police say

children.content: Northern Territory Police believe five-year-old Sharon Granites was abducted from a home in an Alice Springs town camp as the search continues into its third day.

children.content: The world is spending more money on weapons than ever before, reaching nearly $4 trillion in 2025.

items.title: Donald Trump names new ambassador to Australia

children.content: The Trump administration has nominated former congressman David Brat as ambassador to Australia.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit speaks to an audience.

items.title: 'Aggressive' insurers negotiating hospitals to closure, industry says

children.content: A former Bupa executive accuses health insurers of using "almost bullying" tactics in their negotiations with private hospitals, while the Australian Private Hospitals Association says insurers have become "cowboys" in need of regulation.

image.altText: An adult lies in a hospital bed.

items.title: Rebel Wilson accused of being 'utterly dishonest' in defamation trial grilling

children.content: The Hollywood star is being sued for defamation in the Federal Court by Charlotte MacInnes, who plays the lead actor in Ms Wilson's debut directorial film The Deb.

image.altText: Blonde woman in pink and white top smiles

items.title: Coalition points to stealth bombers as potential AUKUS stop-gap

children.content: The Coalition says Australia should consider buying next-generation stealth bombers to help bridge the gap until the AUKUS submarines arrive.

articles.title: UK inquiry finds signs AUKUS investment has 'already faltered'

children.content: The House of Commons Defence Committee releases the findings of its yearlong review into the trilateral defence partnership.

image.altText: A crowd of people standing on a submarine.

articles.title: Weapons maker Thales loses out on multi-billion-dollar missile contract

children.content: Thales was the favourite to win the contract to develop a mobile missile system for Australia, but now the future of its StrikeMaster missile launcher is uncertain.

image.altText: A StrikeMaster vehicle.

items.title: 'System … is broken': Mounjaro won't be listed on PBS in 'stand-off' with US maker

children.content: The "broken" process behind listing medication on the PBS contributes to hundreds of thousands of Australians with diabetes missing out on subsidised lifesaving medication, according to the peak industry body.

items.title: How a $2.2 trillion dream city in the Saudi desert got wound back

children.content: Saudi Arabia's plans to transform its economy were already facing headwinds when the Iran war broke out, placing them even further in jeopardy.

image.altText: NEOM the line light

items.title: Four US presidents have been assassinated but attempts on Trump 'unusual'

children.content: The latest attack on US President Donald Trump has once again thrust his country's long history of political violence into the spotlight.

articles.title: Affidavit reveals more details as man charged with attempted Trump assassination

children.content: The man accused of opening fire at a Washington dinner attended by Donald Trump has been charged with attempting to assassinate the US president and could face life in prison if convicted.

image.altText: man in hat and gown sits on concrete wall

articles.title: Jimmy Kimmel says joke misconstrued as Trumps call to fire late-night host

children.content: Donald Trump says Jimmy Kimmel should be fired for a monologue he delivered days before the third alleged attempt to assassinate the US president.

items.title: They were the first victims of the Port Arthur massacre. Their family has never forgotten

children.content: Seascape guesthouse owners Sally and David Martin were the first people murdered at Port Arthur in 1996. On the 30th anniversary of the tragedy, their granddaughter tells of how the family has refused to be defined by it.

image.altText: A man and woman pose for a photo in front of a waterfall.

items.title: Electric ferry needs a lift as transport ship stuck in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The world's largest electric ferry is sitting idle in Tasmania after construction was completed because the heavy-lift ship that was booked to take it to South America is stuck in the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: New electric incat the China Zorrilla 2025-01-16 11:01:00

items.title: NRL's greatest ever try-scorer hints at imminent PNG switch

children.content: Record-setting South Sydney try-scoring winger Alex Johnston looks set to become the first player to sign for the PNG Chiefs ahead of their 2028 NRL entry.

items.title: ASX loses ground, Bank of Japan keeps rates at 0.75pc

children.content: Australia's share market has lost ground ahead of Wednesday's inflation data. The Bank of Japan has kept the overnight rate at 0.75pc. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

items.title: NSW public transport Opal network is set to change. Here's what we know

children.content: To better understand what respect looks like in practice, I spoke with other First Nations people about how they approach working and living on other mobs' Countries.

items.title: Nedra Talley Ross of the Ronettes, who sang Baby I Love You, dies
